name: El Bao Trail
name_original: Ribera de la Laguna del Rey — circular desde Ruidera
description: Jog El Bao Trail, an easy 2.6-mile (4.2 km) route with 256 feet (78 metres) elevation gain, taking 30 minutes in Ruidera.
region_intro: Jogging around Ruidera offers routes through the unique landscape of the Ruidera Natural Park, characterized by its chain of interconnected lagoons and waterfalls. The terrain is generally gentle, with paths winding alongside the water bodies and through surrounding natural areas. Runners can expect varied surfaces, from well-maintained trails to more natural paths, suitable for different fitness levels. The region provides opportunities for scenic runs with views of the lakes and local flora, making it ideal for summer running routes Ruidera. Best jogging routes around Ruidera The most popular jogging route is El Bao Trail, an easy 2.6 miles (4.2 km) trail that takes 30 minutes to complete. This route offers a straightforward path suitable for beginner jogging trails Ruidera. Another top favourite among local runners is Lagoons in the most beautiful colors – Footbridge Between Two Lagoons loop from Ruidera, a moderate 9.3 miles (14.9 km) path. This running route provides scenic views of the interconnected lagoons, ideal for jogging routes Ruidera with viewpoints. Local runners also love the Laguna del Rey – Laguna del Rey – Ruidera loop from Laguna Redondilla, a 12.5 miles (20.1 km) trail leading through the diverse landscape of the Ruidera lakes, often completed in about 3 hours 40 minutes. Jogging around Ruidera is defined by its chain of lagoons, natural park trails, and gentle elevations. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy jogging routes near Ruidera to more challenging running trails near Ruidera. The routes in Ruidera are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 4 reviews. More than 350 runners have used komoot to explore Ruidera's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Ruidera?
A: There are over 20 running routes around Ruidera, offering a variety of options for different fitness levels. These trails wind through the unique landscape of the Ruidera Natural Park, characterized by its chain of interconnected lagoons and waterfalls.

Q: Are there easy running paths for beginners in Ruidera?
A: Yes, Ruidera offers several easy jogging routes suitable for beginners. A great option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1210380750/el-bao-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Bao Trail&lt;/a&gt;, which is an easy 2.6 miles (4.2 km) and takes about 30 minutes to complete. This route provides a straightforward path alongside the water.

Q: What are some challenging running trails in Ruidera?
A: For those seeking a more challenging run, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31450092" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna del Rey – Laguna del Rey – Ruidera loop from Laguna Redondilla&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 12.5 miles (20.1 km) trail. It leads through diverse landscapes of the Ruidera lakes and typically takes around 3 hours 40 minutes to complete. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39559515" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna del Rey – Laguna del Rey – Ruidera loop from Ruidera&lt;/a&gt;, which spans 15.1 miles (24.3 km) with significant elevation changes.

Q: Are there circular running routes in Ruidera?
A: Yes, many of the running routes in Ruidera are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. A popular choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31448627" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lagoons in the most beautiful colors – Footbridge Between Two Lagoons loop from Ruidera&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 9.3 miles (14.9 km) path offering scenic views of the interconnected lagoons.

Q: What can I see along the jogging routes in Ruidera?
A: The running routes in Ruidera offer stunning natural sights, primarily the chain of lagoons and waterfalls within the Natural Park. You can expect to see highlights such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5573792" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna del Rey&lt;/a&gt;, the beautiful &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3844258" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Batanas Lagoon Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, and the vibrant &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/322233" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lagoons in the most beautiful colors&lt;/a&gt;. Many trails provide excellent viewpoints over these natural wonders.

Q: Are there running trails with water access in Ruidera?
A: Given that Ruidera is defined by its chain of interconnected lagoons, many running trails offer direct views and access to the water. The paths often wind alongside the lakes, providing opportunities to enjoy the water features and even spot local wildlife.

Q: What do other runners enjoy the most about jogging in Ruidera?
A: The running trails in Ruidera are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Runners frequently praise the unique landscape of the Natural Park, the scenic views of the lagoons, and the varied terrain that caters to different abilities. Over 350 runners have used komoot to explore the area.

Q: What is the best time of year to go running in Ruidera?
A: Ruidera is particularly ideal for summer running routes due to its numerous lagoons and natural shade along some paths. However, the mild climate of the region generally makes it suitable for jogging throughout spring and autumn as well, when temperatures are pleasant and the natural scenery is vibrant.

Q: Are there family-friendly jogging routes in Ruidera?
A: Yes, several routes are suitable for families, especially those looking for easier, shorter distances. The generally gentle terrain and well-maintained trails make it possible for families to enjoy a run together, with options like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1210380750/el-bao-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Bao Trail&lt;/a&gt; being a good starting point.

Q: Are there dog-friendly running paths in Ruidera?
A: Many of the natural trails in Ruidera Natural Park are suitable for running with dogs, provided they are kept on a leash and you adhere to park regulations regarding pet waste. It's always recommended to check specific signage at trailheads for any local restrictions.

Q: Where can I find parking for jogging routes around the Ruidera lakes?
A: Parking is generally available at various access points to the Ruidera Natural Park and near the main lagoons. Specific parking areas can often be found close to popular trailheads, especially around towns like Ruidera itself, providing convenient access to the running routes.

Q: How long are the running trails in Ruidera?
A: The running trails in Ruidera vary significantly in length. You can find shorter routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1210380750/el-bao-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Bao Trail&lt;/a&gt; at 2.6 miles (4.2 km), perfect for a quick run, up to longer, more challenging paths such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39559515" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna del Rey – Laguna del Rey – Ruidera loop from Ruidera&lt;/a&gt;, which extends to 15.1 miles (24.3 km).
tips:
The King's Lagoon, one of the most emblematic lagoons in the Ruidera Lakes Natural Park:
It is located within the municipality of Ruidera (Ciudad Real), at the main entrance to the natural park.

It is one of the first lagoons you see when traveling through the park by road, very close to the town of Ruidera.
It stands out for the turquoise color of its waters, which in some places are reminiscent of the Caribbean.
It is photogenic and easily accessible, ideal for those looking for a place to relax or take a swim.
The bottom is clearly visible thanks to the water's transparency.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to jog this trail?
faqs.answer: The El Bao Trail is designed for jogging and typically takes around 30 minutes to complete. This estimate is based on an average jogging pace for its 4.2 km distance.

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for beginner joggers?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route is considered easy, making it very suitable for beginner joggers. Its relatively short distance and minimal elevation gain provide a gentle introduction to trail jogging.

faqs.question: Where does the El Bao Trail start, and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The trail starts from Ruidera, as indicated by its full name 'Ribera de la Laguna del Rey circular desde Ruidera'. While specific parking areas are not detailed, you can generally find parking within the town of Ruidera or near the access points to the Laguna del Rey area.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to jog this trail?
faqs.answer: The best time to jog this trail is typically during the spring and autumn months. Temperatures are milder, and the natural scenery is vibrant. Summer can be very hot, so early mornings or late evenings are recommended if jogging during that season.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on the trail?
faqs.answer: Generally, dogs are allowed on trails within natural parks like Parque Natural de las Lagunas de Ruidera, but they must be kept on a leash at all times. This helps protect local wildlife and ensures a pleasant experience for all visitors.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee to access the trail or the Parque Natural de las Lagunas de Ruidera?
faqs.answer: Access to the Parque Natural de las Lagunas de Ruidera and its trails, including the El Bao Trail, typically does not require an entrance fee or permit for general use. However, always check the official park regulations for any specific activities or updated rules.

faqs.question: What can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: The route offers scenic views, primarily along the banks of the Laguna del Rey. You'll experience the natural beauty of the Ribera de la Laguna del Rey, which is part of the larger Lagunas de Ruidera natural park.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with other paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the El Bao Trail route incorporates sections of several official paths. You will find yourself on parts of the Sendero El Bao, Cañada Conquense o De Los Serranos, Camino Natural del Guadiana, and various segments of the Camino del Margen Izquierdo.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect while jogging?
faqs.answer: Given its 'easy' difficulty and designation for jogging, the terrain is likely to be relatively flat and well-maintained. Expect a mix of natural surfaces, possibly compacted earth or gravel, suitable for comfortable jogging shoes.

faqs.question: Is the El Bao Trail a loop or an out-and-back route?
faqs.answer: The full name of the route, 'Ribera de la Laguna del Rey circular desde Ruidera', indicates that it is a loop trail. This means you will start and end at the same point without retracing your steps.

faqs.question: What should I bring with me when jogging this trail?
faqs.answer: For this relatively short and easy jogging trail, essential items include comfortable jogging shoes, appropriate athletic clothing, and a water bottle to stay hydrated. Sun protection (hat, sunscreen) is also advisable, especially during sunnier months.
